meta.title: Talking Flower Web · Chatty Companion & Hourly Chime
meta.description: A free web talking flower that chatters on its own, chimes every hour, and reads out your reminders aloud. Open in any browser — no install, works on mobile.

hero.lead: A cheerful little flower that lives in your browser tab. It chit-chats on its own, announces the time every hour, and calls out the reminders you set. Free, no install, works on desktop and mobile.
hero.wakeNote: Browsers need one tap before a page can play sound.
hero.pokeHint: Poke the flower and it talks back.

items.title: Auto chatter
items.body: Hundreds of original lines in English, Chinese and Japanese. The flower speaks up every few minutes so your tab never feels lonely — pick chatty, normal or quiet.

items.title: Hourly chime
items.body: A friendly voice announces the time at the top of every hour. Toggle it on or off any time.

items.title: Spoken reminders
items.body: Add a time and a note; the flower reads it out loud and pops a desktop notification. Repeat daily or just once.

items.title: Your own lines
items.body: Write your own things for the flower to say. They mix into the random pool, kept separately per language.

items.title: Cute voices
items.body: Auto-picks the best-sounding voice on your device, with Cute / Normal / Calm presets plus speed and pitch sliders.

items.title: Night rest
items.body: Set wake and sleep hours. The flower stays quiet at night — reminders still fire — and greets you good morning and good night.

what.heading: What is a talking flower?
body: A talking flower is a playful desktop companion: a little animated flower that speaks out loud on its own. This web version is inspired by the beloved chatty-flower toy, rebuilt to run entirely in your browser — no gadget, no batteries, no account.

Everything happens on your device. The flower uses your browser's built-in text-to-speech to talk, a local clock for the hourly chime, and your own list for reminders. There is no microphone, no server, and nothing is uploaded.
It is equal parts toy and tiny utility: cute enough to keep on a second screen, useful enough to nudge you through the day.

steps.title: Wake the flower
steps.body: Tap “Wake the flower” once. That unlocks sound — browsers require a click — and the flower says hello.

steps.title: Choose a voice
steps.body: Open Settings, pick a voice (or leave it on Auto), and try the Cute preset for a higher, sweeter tone.

steps.title: Turn on what you want
steps.body: Switch on the hourly chime and auto-chatter, and set your wake / sleep hours to enable night rest.

steps.title: Add reminders & lines
steps.body: Add a reminder like “09:30 stand up” and write a few lines of your own. Keep the tab open and the flower does the rest.

items.title: Company while you work
items.body: Gentle, random chit-chat makes a quiet room or a long focus session feel less lonely.

items.title: A time cue you don't watch
items.body: The hourly chime keeps you loosely aware of time without staring at a clock.

items.title: Reminders that reach you
items.body: Spoken aloud plus a notification is much harder to miss than a silent badge.

items.title: Simple and private
items.body: No install, no login, nothing leaves your browser. Just open a tab.

items.a: Yes, completely free. It runs in your browser using built-in speech, with no account and no payment.
Only to load the page. After that the flower, clock and reminders all work offline; the voice is your device's built-in text-to-speech.
Browsers block audio until you interact with the page. Tap “Wake the flower” once, and check that your system and the tab are not muted.

items.q: Do reminders work if I close the tab?
items.a: No — the web version needs the tab open. For always-on background reminders, use the browser extension version.

items.q: Can I change the voice or make it cuter?
items.a: Yes. Settings lets you pick any voice your device offers, with Cute / Normal / Calm presets and speed and pitch sliders. Microsoft Edge offers especially natural free voices.

items.q: Is this an official Nintendo product?
items.a: No. It is a fan-made web toy, not affiliated with or endorsed by Nintendo. All voice lines are original.
English, Chinese and Japanese — both the interface and the flower's spoken lines.

footer.disclaimer: Fan-made web toy. Not affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by Nintendo. All voice lines are original; speech uses your browser's built-in TTS.

ui.autoChatterDesc: The flower says something on its own once in a while
ui.hourlyChimeDesc: Announces the time at the top of every hour
ui.quietNightDesc: No chatter or chime during sleep hours (reminders still fire)
ui.customDesc: Add your own chatter lines — they get mixed into the random pool.
ui.useBuiltinDesc: Turn off to make the flower say only your lines
ui.linePlaceholder: A line for the flower to say…
ui.customEmpty: No custom lines yet — add one!
ui.plansDesc: The flower reads it aloud and shows a notification. Keep this tab open; use the extension for background reminders.
